sourceMovie.title: The Jackal
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Jackal (1997) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Assassins
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Jackal clicked for you, Assassins (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum

faq.question: What should I watch after The Jackal?
faq.answer: Try Assassins, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, Run All Night—each shares DNA with The Jackal while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Jackal?
faq.answer: The Jackal (1997) is best described as Action, Thriller, Adventure, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Jackal worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
